The All stars representing the National League affiliates won 8-5. Richmond Braves catcher and first baseman Luis Lopez won the top award for the International League. Playoffs Division Series The Syracuse Chiefs won the East Division Finals over the Pawtucket Red Sox, 3 games to 1. texts.full_text: e roots serve as a pathognomonic sign strongly indicative of Neurofibromatosis type 1 (NF-1). These tumors often cause enlargement of the intervertebral foramen by growing around the nerve exits. The degree of this enlargement varies: if most of the tumor is located outside the spinal canal, only minimal widening occurs at the intervertebral foramina. Conversely, when a significant portion lies within the canal, more pronounced enlargement may result. This understanding underscores the importance of imaging techniques like MRI in assessing tumor location and extent,

texts.full_text: the time zone offset doesn't provide enough information for us determine which specific timezone that corresponds with an arbitrary number of minutes. However what we can do instead, given you have UTC date and your client’s local hours difference (offset), convert them into a DateTimeOffset object. This will give both datetime as well its corresponding Offset from Coordinated Universal Time(UTC). texts.full_text: know that because there's a priest init—and he’s talking about how faith and religion are intertwined but distinct aspects of spiritual life. The original "Jesus > Religion" clip, which went massively viral on social media platforms like YouTube and Facebook, presents the argument from Christian leaders as Judah Smith at The City Church that Jesus’ message is often misunderstood or overshadowed by religious institutions. It emphasizes a personal relationship with Christ over adherence to institutionalized religion. In this Catholic response video, however, Father Robert Barron of Word on Fire Ministries takes up the conversation and adds nuance. He acknowledges some valid points made in "Jesus > Religion" but also argues that while Jesus indeed calls for an intimate spiritual connection beyond mere rituals or rules, organized faith communities play a crucial role by providing structure, tradition, sacraments, community support—and most importantly—a deeper understanding of Scripture. texts.full_text: e of time (10 percent), 10 percent (lack of interest) and too much drama (nine percent).Facebook guru Mark Zuckerberg doesn’t need to worry about whether I’m less of a Facebook fan than I was a year or two ago. He should care that my 17-year-old daughter can’t remember the last time that she was on Facebook. She’s not a big fan of the surging crop of ads on Facebook.The same applies to her friends, who do most of their electronic communications through instantmessaging and mobile texting apps like WhatsApp or Kik Messenger. They find these platforms more convenient for qUick conversations and easier to navigate than the increasingly complex social network that is Facebook. The younger demographic's preference could indicate a shift in how future generations will engage with social media, emphasizing real-time communication tools over traditional status update feeds. This trend aligns with people's growing concerns about privacy on such platforms as well as their dissatisfaction due to information overload and perceived time wasting activities they entail. Moreover, the decline of Facebook usage among certain demographics might also reflect a fatigue from relentless social comparison which often leads individuals feeling inadequate or unhappy when seeing only others successes or exciting events - leading towards "keeping up with Jones" syndrome metaphorically speaking- without necessarily providing space for deeper conversations and connections one might desire in these mediums. Furthermore, let's not forget about Zuckerberg's infamous IPO where he had been criticized heavily over his handling of share offerings; perhaps people have become wary about giving too much personal information due to such incidents which highlight corporate power dynamics at play here as well? But isn’t this a double-edged sword? texts.full_text: Sky's offices in Leeds Dock. Doors will open at 6:30. The meeting will be in SkyWe're excited to welcome you to our upcoming Python Dojo event on Thursday, May 9th, 2019. This month, we've arranged a meetup at Sky's offices in Leeds Dock, and we're thrilled to have the opportunity to host you there. The doors will open promptly at 6:30 PM, giving you ample time to settle in and prepare for an evening of engaging Python discussions and hands-on learning. Our event is designed to foster a collaborative environment where Python enthusiasts can share knowledge, explore new concepts, and tackle real-world challenges together. Sky's offices provide a comfortable and inspiring setting for our Dojo sessions. Whether you're an experienced Python developer looking to refine your skills or a beginner eager to embark on your journey in Python programming, we're confident that this event will offer something valuable for everyone. Please make sure to arrive on time so that you can take full advantage of the evening's activities. We'll kick off with a brief introduction and overview of the topics we'll be covering. Following this, we'll dive into interactive coding sessions, problem-solving challenges, and Q&A discussions. Don't forget to bring your laptops or notebooks; you'll have the chance to work on real-world projects and collaborate with fellow attendees. Additionally, we encourage you to share your own experiences and insights, making this event a truly collective learning experience. We look forward to seeing you at Sky's offices in Leeds Dock on May

texts.full_text: from the Ancient Egyptian Village of Deir el-Medina [Willeke, 2013, p145]. The workers of this village were particularly skilled artisans, and children were apprenticed to other families in the village to learn their skills. In the later periods, we have a pretty good overall understanding of the apprentice system that was in force in ancient Egypt during the Roman period. Once again, children seem to have been apprenticed locally to learn their skills. This does not mean that their training was necessarily carried out locally. Smiths and potters would certainly have been based locally. The same is probably true for boat-builders. However, masons, for example, might travel considerable distances to work on commissions, and their apprentices would accompany them. A good, accessible paper here would be W. L. Westermann's Apprentice Contracts and the Apprentice System in Roman Egypt. Although not really artisans in the traditional sense, the only "trade" that the ancient Egyptians seem to have travelled any great distance to learn is that of the scribe. There is some good evidence for scribal training being carried out in a town or city that was not the apprentice's home town. Ronald J. Williams' 1972 paper Scribal Training in Ancient Egypt is available to read free online if you'd like more information. Sources Wendrich Willeke (ed.): Archaeology and Apprenticeship: Body Knowledge, Identity, and Communities of Practice, University of Arizona, 2013 Westermann, W. L: Apprent

texts.full_text: advertisements for hormone replacement therapy. This article examines and presents examples of contemporary advertising within the medical and health professions that continue the process and organisation of knowledge about women and their reproductive bodies. It draws on feminist and poststructural perspectives to inform a critical evaluation of the visual representations of menopausal women and hormone replacement therapy. These representations work to construct certain definitions of the feminine that sustain and support existing contradictory cultural meanings and values abo...t towards womanhood, femininity, and reproductive life in society. This article examines how advertisements continue to perpetuate traditional notions of beauty, youthfulness, and femininity when representing menopausal women undergoing hormone replacement therapy. Through a critical discourse analysis of contemporary medical advertising, this study reveals the ways in which visual representations reinforce societal expectations around aging, embodiment, and womanhood during perimenopause. The research draws on feminist poststructuralist theory to explore how these advertisements construct certain definitions of femininity that are tied to dominant cultural meanings surrounding reproductive life. texts.full_text: nemosis in Manipuri ponies in India. Information pertaining to parasitic fauna and parasitic diseases in Manipuri ponies in India is not available. Moreover, no systematic studies have been undertaken on cutaneous habronemosis in Manipuri ponies which is a common skin problem of Manipuri ponies as reported by pony owners. Keeping in the view of the importance of parasitic infections in veterinary health coverage particularly in Manipuri ponies, the present study was planned. A survey of natural cases of cutaneous habronemosis followed by molecular confirmation of species involved and treatments were done. Out of 200 ponies examined, nine cases (4.5%) of cutaneous habronemosis was recorded. Gross examination revealed raised and ulcerated wounds with necrotic tissues covered with granulation. Histopathological study revealed granuloma and in the center of the granuloma with necrotic debris. Remnants of the larvae and eggs of and spp. were identified. Molecular analysis of the larvae and eggs confirmed the presence of and as the primary causative agents. Treatment of the ponies involved the topical application of ivermectin and the use of an antiseptic wound spray. Additionally, the ponies were administered systemic ivermectin. texts.full_text: count I'm trying to wrap my head around what counts against the API call quota. I have a page with a map, the map has 5000 locations marked on it. Does google count the 1 page load or the 5000 locations it marked on the map? A: You can find the answer to this in the new documentation Understanding Billing for Maps, Routes, and Places From the SKU: Dynamic Maps A web page or application that displays a map using the Maps JavaScript API. A map is created with the google.maps.Map() class. User interactions with the map, such as panning, zooming, or switching map layers, do not generate additional map loads. However, if you create multiple maps on a single page (e.g., using multiple instances of the google.maps.Map() class), each map is counted as a separate map load. In your case, since you have 5000 locations marked on the map, it's likely that Google Maps API counts this as one map load for the initial page load. The 5000 markers are part of the map data and do not generate additional map loads. However, if you're using other APIs like Places or Directions to fetch data for those markers, each request would be counted separately against their respective quotas. To confirm, here's what counts as a single map load: * One instance of the google.maps.Map() class on your page. * User interactions with that map (e.g., panning, zooming). * Adding or removing markers, polygons, etc. does not count as additional map loads. What would count against other quotas are requests

texts.full_text: the many different crystal phases that ice has at different pressures. Wald discusses the interior metric in section 6.2 of his book General Relativity, but even there he treats only the case of a perfect fluid. The derivation takes many pages so I won't reproduce it here. I'll give only the final result, which is: $$ ds^2 = -e^{2\phi}dt^2 + \left(1 -\frac{2m(r)}{r}\right)^{-1}dr^2 + d\Omega^2 $$ The mass function $m(r)$ is the mass within the radius $r$ and is given by: $$ m(r) = 4\pi \int_0^r \rho(r')r'^2 \,dr' $$ The time function $\phi$ is given by: $$ \frac{d\phi}{dr} = \frac{m(r) + 4\pi r^3 P}{r(r - 2m(r))} $$ To calculate the time dilation you would need to specify the properties of your matter and use those properties to (probably numerically) calculate $\phi$. texts.full_text: subjects are under threat of an aversive event (electric shock), and that light reflex amplitude correlates negatively with subjective anxiety. Furthermore, we have shown that the threat-induced reduction in light reflex amplitude is sensitive to the effect of the anxiolytic drug diazepam. We have suggested that the 'fear-inhibited light reflex' paradigm could be used as a laboratory model of human anxiety. In the present study, we examined whether a single oral dose (200 microg) of the sedative-sympatholytic drug clonidine would antagonize the effects of threat on the pupillary light reflex. Twelve healthy male volunteers participated in two sessions separated by seven days in which they ingested clonidine 200 mg or placebo in a double-blind, balanced, cross-over design. Light stimuli (0.43 mW/cm2, 200 msec) were generated by a green (peak wavelength 565 nm) light-emitting diode, and pupil diameter was monitored by computerized binocular infrared television pupillometry in the dark. The light reflex response was recorded during either the anticipation of a shock ('threat' blocks) or periods in which no shocks were anticipated ('safe' blocks). The shock was a single square wave current (15 mA, 20 msec duration), delivered through surface electrodes positioned on the volar aspect of the left wrist. Pupil diameter increased and light reflex amplitude decreased during threat compared with safe conditions both before and after clonidine administration. However, there were no significant differences between these two baseline measurements for either pupil diameter or light reflex response (p > 0.25). These data demonstrate that a single oral dose of the sedative-antihypertensive drug clonidine has no effect on pupillary changes induced by threat. PMID: 17846389 [PubMed -

texts.full_text: "horse of the water" in Scottish Gaelic, is indeed a formidable and malevolent creature in Irish and Scottish folklore. While it shares some similarities with the Kelpie—a mythical water horse often associated with freshwater lakes and rivers—the each uisge is considered more sinister. Unlike the Kelpie, which is known to lure humans onto its back before drowning them, the each uisge is said to have a deadly kiss that can drain life from its victims. It also has a trickster's nature and can take various forms, often appearing as an attractive human or horse to entice unsuspecting travelers. The Aughisky, as it is known in Ireland, inhabits lakes and lochans (small lakes), particularly those with islands at their centers. According to legend, these creatures are the souls of dead druids or other unbaptized beings who cannot find rest and transform into these fearsome beasts. The each uisge's danger lies not only in its physical power but also in its ability to manipulate humans through deception. Its presence serves as a cautionary tale about trusting app

texts.full_text: for David Wong Rubin “The Hurricane” Carter sat in prison for over 20 years for murders he did not commit, until he was finally freed due to the work of dedicated activists. He had his life portrayed on the big screen in “The Hurricane.” Some of you may have seen the it and reveled in the ending where Carter is freed, but did you know that we also have our own “Hurricane” story? Like Hurricane Carter, David Wong sits in a New York prison cell convicted of a crime he did not commit.He was accused of murdering a prison inmate, although he never met the victim, was nowhere near him when the killing occurred, and was not given a translator in his dialect. The conviction rested primarily on the testimony of self-interested criminals. The sad story is that many poor immigrants like Wong are channeled through the criminal justice system without the full benefits of competent counsel and translators. The David WWong Support Committee, along with other activists and organizations, has been diligently working to free him. They argue that his conviction was based on flawed evidence and testimonies filled with inconsistencies. Despite language barriers and inadequate legal representation during the trial process where Mandarin or Cantonese interpreters were used instead of someone fluent Toisanese—Wong's native dialect, he maintained a consistent story: He had never met victim nor been near him at time crime occurred yet found himself convicted primarily through testimony from criminals seeking favors. Advocates for Wong highlight several troubling aspects about his case: The lack of physical evidence linking Wongs to the murder, questionable reliability regarding witness testimonies used against David along with inadequate defense due language barriers among others issues have led many believe that there has been an egregious miscarriage justice. Supporters continue fighting for his exo-neration, similar how Carter's advocates did not give up until they saw him freed. They host rallies, circulate petitions and engage with lawmakers while raising awareness about broader problems faced by non English speaking defendants within US legal system hoping that one day soon justice will prevail for David

HIV kills or damages cells in the body's immune system, gradually destroying the body's ability to fight infection and certain cancers. CDC estimates that 1.1 million people are living with HIV/AIDS in the United States. People with HIV/AIDS are considered at increased risk from serious influenza-related complications. Studies have shown an increased risk for heart- and lung-related hospitalizations in people infected with HIV during influenza season as opposed to other times of the year, and a higher risk of influenza-related death in HIV-infected people. HIV-positive individuals, especially those with a low CD4 cell count, are more susceptible to the complications of influenza, and the influenza virus can exacerbate the symptoms and complications of HIV, leading to a more rapid progression of HIV-related diseases. texts.full_text: ears pinned back, a nose job, longer hair, liposuction, my teeth fixed, my forehead reduced and even an eyelift to make my eyes look bigger. Remember, this all took place in my 9-year-old brain. The thought of all that "self-correction" still appeals to me to this day, but I know it won’t make the BDD go away. The problems that actually need fixing reside in a place much deeper than my love handles and ears that stick out. Depression I first time I realized there was something wrong was when I was 19 years old, just after I’d moved out of my home and gone to college. As I was starting to mature and become a "real adult," I accepted my behavior and thought processes were not healthy. As the depression and anxiety became more debilitating, I knew it was time to admit I had a problem. I started to take anti-depressants and engaged in cognitive behavioral therapy. Three years later, I am still taking anti-depressants and still engaging in therapy. I am in no way cured, and to be honest, I doubt I ever will be. But, it does get easier. I have made so much progress throughout the last three years and I am determined to continue to do just that. I can’t help thinking that if I could have identified my problem earlier, the chances of a full recovery — or, at least a quicker one — would have been better. texts.full_text: had stage I disease; of these, seven patients died during follow up due to causes other than cancer. Among the remaining second-stage tumors, there were three deaths from thyroid carcinoma and two other deaths caused by factors unrelated to any neoplasm. All third- or fourth-stage malignancies showed progression even though their tumors had been considered nonmetastatic (T3NxM0) at presentation. Overall survival was remarkably dependent on TNM stage at diagnosis: 95% for first, 71% for second and none for the two patients with metastatic disease at initial staging (fourth-stage). Grade of differentiation was associated with overall survival in multivariate analysis; no cases of death were found among well-differentiated tumors. A statistically significant association between p53 expression and survival was also demonstrated: all six subjects who died during follow up had tumors that expressed p53, whereas none who survived expressed this oncoprotein. Multivariate Cox's regression model showed that TNM stage at presentation (P=0.02) and p53 accumulation in tumor tissue ( P<0.01), but not proliferative activity or surgery outcome were significant predictors of five-year survival. CONCLUSION: Our results confirm that clinical stage is a more powerful predictor of overall survival than other factors such as histology, histopathological subtype, serum thyroglobulin levels during the first year after treatment and surgical procedure. The presence of p53 protein immunostaining at diagnosis was demonstrated to be an independent prognostic factor for thyroid cancer patients treated with surgery; this marker may contribute in predicting progression in disease behavior when combined with TNM staging information. Prospective evaluation of pet/ct using 18fdg or fdg and methionine in the characterization of pulmonary nodules: comparison with bronchoscopy, thoracotomy, and ct findings.
